name: Romance
core_pattern: The hero succeeds in a quest, triumphing over adversity and achieving an idealized world
typical_arc: Hero sets out → faces trials → defeats antagonist → harmony restored
examples: Arthurian legend, The Lord of the Rings, fairy tales
tension_signature: Good vs. evil, purity vs. corruption, freedom vs. captivity

name: Comedy
core_pattern: Society moves from a state of confusion or repression to a harmonious new order, often via marriage or social integration
typical_arc: Rigid old order → comic complications → young lovers or outsiders prevail → inclusive new society
examples: A Midsummer Night's Dream, Pride and Prejudice, most romantic comedies
tension_signature: Individual desire vs. social convention, flexibility vs. rigidity

name: Tragedy
core_pattern: An exceptional individual falls from a position of greatness due to a fatal flaw or circumstance, isolated from society
typical_arc: Hero at height of power → hamartia or hubris emerges → reversal (peripeteia) → catastrophic fall → isolation or death
tension_signature: Individual will vs. fate, ambition vs. limitation, loyalty vs. self-interest

name: Irony / Satire
core_pattern: Reality fails to match ideals; the heroic or romantic is deflated; the world is exposed as absurd, corrupt, or indifferent
typical_arc: Apparent normality → gap between appearance and reality widens → exposure of futility or hypocrisy → no redemption or only ironic resolution
tension_signature: Ideal vs. reality, appearance vs. truth, hope vs. futility
